articles.headline: Why Do US Airlines Still Use The Boeing 757 On Key Routes?
articles.blurb: Dozens of 757s still fly to this day, but four decades after the aircraft's introduction, why is this?
articles.takeaway: The 757's unmatched range-to-capacity ratio and proven economics on thin transatlantic routes make it irreplaceable for US carriers despite age, as modern narrow-body alternatives lack comparable performance.

articles.headline: Tight Space: Inside The B-2 Spirit Cockpit
articles.blurb: The B-2 Spirit has to perform extra-long missions, while providing less space and comfort for its pilots than transport aircraft like the C-17.
articles.takeaway: B-2 pilots endure extreme spatial constraints and comfort limitations across 30+ hour missions, requiring specialized training and physiological resilience beyond typical fighter pilot standards.

articles.headline: Press Release: Aer Lingus takes off with Starlink inflight Wi-Fi
articles.blurb: Aer Lingus has equipped the first aircraft in its fleet with Starlink, delivering ultrafast, reliable Wi-Fi to customers and marking a major step forward for customer experience. Customers travelling from Dublin to New York JFK on flight EI105 today are the first to experience the innovative...
articles.takeaway: Aer Lingus's Starlink deployment signals satellite-based connectivity is transitioning from niche offering to mainstream transatlantic service, potentially accelerating industry-wide adoption beyond traditional air-to-ground systems.

articles.headline: Unidentified Drones Crash Near Kouvola, Finland, Authorities Investigate Possible Origin
articles.blurb: Finnish authorities are investigating reports that one or two unmanned aerial systems havecrashed in southeastern...
articles.takeaway: Finland's drone crash investigation highlights growing vulnerability of critical infrastructure to unattributed UAV incidents amid tensions with neighboring Russia.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Safety risk management effectiveness hinges on translating analytical findings into practical control measures while strategically accepting unavoidable residual risks.

articles.headline: Op-Ed: Seating architecture must evolve to accommodate power, IFEC
articles.blurb: Please enjoy this Op-Ed contribution from Unum VP Business Development, Alan McInnes Moving beyond the traditional considerations of structure, comfort and certification, aircraft seating is a critical integration point for power, in-flight entertainment (IFE) and connectivity systems that...
articles.takeaway: Modern aircraft seats must function as integrated infrastructure hubs for power and connectivity, not just passive passenger furniture, requiring coordinated design between seating manufacturers and avionics suppliers.

articles.headline: The One Metric That Matters Most In Premium Economy
articles.blurb: Premium economy has grown significantly in popularity over the past decade.
articles.takeaway: Premium economy's profitability hinges on seat pitch and width optimization, as incremental pricing power depends more on physical space than service amenities.

articles.headline: Southwest Airlines Adds 42 New Nonstop Routes This Spring [Map]
articles.blurb: They include four new cities, with one in particular being historic...
articles.takeaway: Southwest's expansion into four new cities signals the carrier's confidence in point-to-point demand recovery and willingness to compete directly with legacy carriers in underserved markets.

articles.headline: Why Is Airbus Creating A New A220?
articles.blurb: Airbus is moving ahead with plans for an updated A220.
articles.takeaway: Airbus is refreshing the A220 to compete with Boeing's 737 MAX and 787 upgrades, signaling intensifying pressure in the narrow-body market.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Strong safety culture directly influences SMS effectiveness by encouraging voluntary hazard reporting, which identifies risks before they cause incidents rather than after.

articles.headline: 6 Aircraft That Represent The Future Of Air Combat
articles.blurb: Discover six aircraft redefining modern air warfare, from stealth fighters to missile trucks, shaping the balance of air power in 2026 and beyond.
articles.takeaway: Next-generation combat aircraft prioritize multi-role capabilities and sensor fusion over traditional air-to-air dominance, fundamentally shifting military operational strategy.

articles.headline: Former United Boeing 747 Literally Wedged Between Two Seattle Skyscrapers
articles.blurb: A decommissioned Boeing 747 will be suspended between two high-rise apartment buildings in downtown Seattle as part of an art project. This cool installation is set to hang around 14 feet (4.2 meters) above the ground between the two towers of the WB 1200 Project, located at 1200 Stewart Street in...
articles.takeaway: Retired aircraft are finding new value as architectural art installations, offering creative adaptive reuse solutions for decommissioned commercial jets beyond traditional scrapping or storage.

articles.headline: Breaking The ICE: Passenger Hands Out Vodka Shots As Federal Agents Man TSA Lines
articles.blurb: While ICE agents are handing out water across airports, one passenger at a US airport is pouring out vodka shots for fellow passengers!
articles.takeaway: TSA checkpoint disruptions from ICE operations are creating unusual passenger interactions that highlight operational strain on airport security procedures during resource reallocation.

articles.headline: Rare US "Eye In The Sky" Command Plane Destroyed In Iran Strike On Saudi Air Base
articles.blurb: The US has just a handful of E-3 Sentry's in mission-capable condition.
articles.takeaway: Loss of a critical E-3 Sentry reduces US airborne early warning capacity at a time when the military operates with minimal redundancy in this aging fleet.

articles.headline: Just 2 Hours: The World's Shortest Airbus A380 Route Gets Even More Flights
articles.blurb: Two different carriers fly their superjumbos on this corridor.
articles.takeaway: Multiple carriers deploying A380s on ultra-short routes signals sustained profitability on high-capacity corridor travel despite fuel inefficiency concerns.

articles.headline: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
articles.blurb: While NASA outlined plans to increase the cadence of robotic lunar lander missions at a recent event, it said little about accelerating work on crewed lunar landers.
articles.takeaway: NASA's focus on accelerating robotic lunar missions masks a potential gap in crewed lunar lander development timelines, creating schedule risk for human exploration objectives.

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

articles.takeaway: Eastern Iowa Airport's diverse traffic mix from trainers to air carriers positions it as a critical regional hub for both pilot development and commercial connectivity in the Midwest.

articles.headline: Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission
articles.blurb: Starfish Space has changed plans for a docking demonstration mission after its original partner backed out.
articles.takeaway: Partnership shifts in on-orbit servicing missions highlight the operational dependencies and risk factors that emerging aerospace companies face during critical demonstrations.

articles.headline: Germany Considering MQ-28A Ghost Bat CCA, Says Defense Minister
articles.blurb: German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius said the MQ-28 Ghost Bat is among the uncrewed aircraft being evaluated to support the Eurofighter Typhoon fighter fleet. Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius said during his visit to Australia on Mar. 27, 2026, that Berlin is considering Boeing Defence...
articles.takeaway: Germany's evaluation of the MQ-28 Ghost Bat signals NATO's shift toward integrating autonomous unmanned combat aircraft with legacy fighter fleets, requiring coordinated operational doctrine across allied air forces.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight planning can simultaneously expand capacity and reduce environmental impact without requiring wholesale fleet replacement.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: USMC F-35Cs and USS Tripoli ARG Arrive in the Middle East
articles.blurb: A month after the start of the hostilities, the U.S. Marine Corps is increasing its presence in the Middle East with the arrival of a F-35C squadron and the USS Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group. Mar. 28, 2026, marks a month since the U.S. and Israel launched Operations Epic Fury and Roaring Lion,...
articles.takeaway: The Marine Corps F-35C deployment signals a shift toward carrier-based fifth-generation air superiority in Middle East operations, enhancing contested airspace capabilities.

articles.headline: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
articles.blurb: The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch still scheduled as soon as April 1.
articles.takeaway: Final crew arrival signals NASA's readiness to execute humanity's return to lunar orbit, marking the culmination of years of integration testing and launch preparation activities.

articles.headline: [Updated] Iranian Attack on Prince Sultan Air Base Reportedly Struck U.S. KC-135s, E-3 Sentry
articles.blurb: Satellite images show signs of an attack at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia, with The Wall Street Journal now reporting that an Iranian missile damaged “several U.S. refueling aircraft.” Satellite images emerged on Mar. 27, 2026, appeared to show signs of damage on an apron used by U.S....
articles.takeaway: Damage to KC-135 tankers and E-3 Sentries at Prince Sultan would significantly degrade U.S. aerial refueling and airborne early warning capabilities in the Middle East theater.

articles.headline: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards

articles.takeaway: Deep space communication infrastructure built over decades by career specialists like Richards is the critical enabler making lunar missions feasible today.

articles.headline: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
articles.blurb: NASA has awarded Intuitive Machines of Houston, $180.4 million to deliver NASA-funded science and technology to the lunar surface as part of the agency’s CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) initiative and Artemis program. This lunar delivery, which includes seven payloads — five of them NASA’s...
articles.takeaway: NASA's $180.4M contract with Intuitive Machines demonstrates the agency's reliance on commercial partners to reduce costs and accelerate Artemis lunar infrastructure deployment beyond crewed missions.

articles.headline: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members
articles.blurb: March 27, 2026 – Washington, D.C.—The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) is pleased to welcome Astrolab and Zeno Power, two innovative companies advancing planetary mobility and reliable energy in extreme environments. […]...
articles.takeaway: The CSF's expansion to include lunar mobility and next-generation power solutions indicates growing industry consolidation around the critical infrastructure needed for sustained commercial space operations.
